title: A Hunka Hunka Burns In Love

jokes.text: I love Chinatown, although I wish they'd stop picking on Tibet Town.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing love for Chinatown
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur complaint about Tibet Town being picked on
jokes.explanation: Clever political commentary disguised as Homer's oblivious observation about ethnic neighborhoods

jokes.text: People buy them when they're small and cute. Then they flush them down the toilet. Uh, yeah. I'll have the shark butt with butt sauce.
jokes.setup: Bart seemingly talking about pets that get flushed
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's ordering disgusting-sounding food
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with gross-out humor typical of Bart's character

jokes.text: Would you like that with the fragrant bee bellies or the cat noses?
jokes.setup: Normal restaurant service question format
jokes.punchline: Offering disgusting animal parts as condiment choices
jokes.explanation: Escalates the restaurant's questionable menu theme with increasingly absurd options

jokes.text: Is there any way we can enhance your dining experience by hurting an animal?
jokes.setup: Polite customer service inquiry
jokes.punchline: Casually offering animal cruelty as a service upgrade
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that subverts customer service politeness with shocking casual cruelty

jokes.text: Geese can be troublesome.
jokes.setup: Reading a fortune cookie expecting wisdom
jokes.punchline: Gets an obvious, mundane observation about geese
jokes.explanation: Subverts fortune cookie expectations with hilariously banal 'wisdom'

jokes.text: A guy outside told me that.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining the fortune is useless common knowledge
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally just heard this 'wisdom' from a random person outside
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - complaining about obvious advice while revealing he got it from a stranger moments ago

jokes.text: Every house has a bathroom.
jokes.setup: Another fortune cookie reading
jokes.punchline: Even more obvious 'fortune' than the geese one
jokes.explanation: Continues the bad fortune theme but less creatively than Homer's

jokes.text: You will be aroused by a shampoo commercial.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he can write better fortunes
jokes.punchline: His 'better' fortune is weirdly specific and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Homer's idea of 'better' fortune reveals his character while being genuinely funny and quotable

jokes.text: I should be in New York writing riddles on Popsicle sticks!
jokes.setup: Fortune cookie writer lamenting his wasted talent
jokes.punchline: His dream job is writing Popsicle stick riddles
jokes.explanation: Deflates artistic pretension by revealing his 'high calling' is equally trivial

jokes.text: Let a frown be your umbrella. They change it to smile. A frown is a much better umbrella than a smile!
jokes.setup: Complaint about fortune being changed
jokes.punchline: Serious analysis of the practical umbrella qualities of facial expressions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal interpretation of metaphorical saying with passionate conviction

jokes.text: The price of stamps will climb ever higher.
jokes.setup: Homer demonstrating his fortune-writing skills
jokes.punchline: Mundane but accurate prediction presented as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Homer's practical but uninsightful 'wisdom' contrasts with expected mystical fortune

jokes.text: Please! Yung Mee was a hack compared to this guy!
jokes.setup: Restaurant owner praising Homer's fortune skills
jokes.punchline: Name 'Yung Mee' sounds like 'young me'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay pun that works both as character name and self-reference

jokes.text: You will invent a humorous toilet lid.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to write fortunes
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific prediction about toilet humor invention
jokes.explanation: Random absurdity typical of Homer's thought process

jokes.text: Your store is being robbed, Apu.
jokes.setup: Homer writing another fortune
jokes.punchline: Fortune addresses specific person in the room about immediate situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of fortune format - breaks fourth wall by addressing present reality

jokes.text: That fortune really nailed me, and my winning ways.
jokes.setup: Barney reading fortune about being a winner
jokes.punchline: Barney believing he's a winner despite being obvious loser
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney delusion - perfectly fits his character's self-awareness deficit

jokes.text: Yarr! I'll enjoy that.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain reading fortune about sea voyage
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic pirate response to obvious professional activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Sea Captain excited about prediction of his regular activity

jokes.text: Ooh! Me sorry! Me no speaky Chiny!
jokes.setup: Waiter asking for tip
jokes.punchline: Burns using fake broken English to avoid tipping
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns cheapness and casual racism, cringey but in character

jokes.text: General Gao, you're a bloodthirsty fool, but your chicken is delectable.
jokes.setup: Burns commenting on his meal
jokes.punchline: Addressing historical figure as if he personally cooked the dish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns sophistication mixed with obliviousness about modern food naming

jokes.text: That was my thumb.
jokes.setup: Burns complimenting someone on their work
jokes.punchline: Reveals Burns was just feeling Smithers' thumb
jokes.explanation: Burns' physical frailty creates accidental intimate moment with Smithers

jokes.text: Well, it's just you and me here, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns excited about finding true love on Flag Day
jokes.punchline: Smithers pointing out he's the only other person present
jokes.explanation: Smithers' hopeful romantic interpretation of the fortune creates awkward moment

jokes.text: No time for jokes, Smithers. Come along. We're going womanizing.
jokes.setup: Smithers' romantic hope
jokes.punchline: Burns obliviously dismisses it to go find women
jokes.explanation: Burns' obliviousness to Smithers' feelings while using old-fashioned term 'womanizing'

jokes.text: So I foreclosed on her mortgage and took her cats.
jokes.setup: Burns on a date trying to make conversation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of destroying someone's life as dinner conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - casually mentioning evil acts as if they're charming anecdotes. The cats detail makes it worse.

jokes.text: I've gone five minutes without saying, 'Well, I never!'
jokes.setup: Gloria expressing delight at conversation with Burns
jokes.punchline: Reveals she usually says old-fashioned expressions constantly
jokes.explanation: Establishes Gloria's character as similarly old-fashioned, explaining their compatibility.

jokes.text: Damn that Pennybags! Between him and Scrooge McDuck, all the best ankle is taken!
jokes.setup: Burns looking for women at a country club
jokes.punchline: Complaining about fictional rich characters stealing women, using 1920s slang 'ankle'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns moment - treating fictional characters as real competition while using outdated slang

jokes.text: I'd always be second place to some kitten stuck in a tree.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining why he can't date a firefighter
jokes.punchline: Jealous of competing with rescue emergencies for attention
jokes.explanation: Burns' petty jealousy extends to emergency rescues, showing his self-centered nature

jokes.text: This has been the worst Flag Day ever.
jokes.setup: Burns' romantic disappointment
jokes.punchline: Treating Flag Day as if it's normally a major holiday for him
jokes.explanation: Burns' dramatic reaction implies he usually has great Flag Days, absurd expectation

jokes.text: Now, see here, Flatfoot!
jokes.setup: Burns angry at getting a parking ticket
jokes.punchline: Using 1930s gangster slang 'flatfoot' for police officer
jokes.explanation: Burns' antiquated vocabulary shows his age and detachment from modern times

jokes.text: You can lift my wiper any day.
jokes.setup: Burns flirting with the police officer
jokes.punchline: Awkward innuendo about windshield wipers
jokes.explanation: Burns' attempt at smooth talk comes out as awkward car-related innuendo

jokes.text: O frabjous day! She said yes!
jokes.setup: Burns excited about getting a date
jokes.punchline: Using Lewis Carroll nonsense word 'frabjous' in celebration
jokes.explanation: Burns' literary vocabulary (Jabberwocky reference) shows his education and eccentricity

jokes.text: He'll pick you up at 7:00. Wear a petticoat.
jokes.setup: Smithers making date arrangements
jokes.punchline: Demanding 1800s undergarment for modern date
jokes.explanation: Burns' antiquated expectations expressed through Smithers' instructions

jokes.text: I can't stand to see a man single. Some people enjoy being alone, Mom. No. Everyone should be paired up.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing happiness about Burns finding love
jokes.punchline: Absolute rejection of Lisa's reasonable point about personal choice
jokes.explanation: Marge's traditional values clash with modern independence, typical mother behavior

jokes.text: Well, I've never dated anyone with their original hair and teeth.
jokes.setup: Gloria mentioning never dating someone who knew Calvin Coolidge
jokes.punchline: Burns' counter about original body parts
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal - Burns acknowledging his extreme age while pointing out her youth

jokes.text: You're a nice guy, Monty. You're always laughing and tenting your fingers.
jokes.setup: Gloria complimenting Burns
jokes.punchline: Identifying his signature villain gesture as endearing trait
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Burns' signature villain pose being seen as cute quirk

jokes.text: I really feel safe with you. It's like going out with my brother.
jokes.setup: Gloria explaining why she likes Burns
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to her brother, romantically devastating
jokes.explanation: Classic friend-zone line delivered with oblivious enthusiasm

jokes.text: like piloting motor coaches and collecting dog waste.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming to be into youth trends
jokes.punchline: His examples are driving buses and picking up dog poop
jokes.explanation: Burns' complete disconnect from youth culture - thinks menial jobs are trends

jokes.text: It's not important how old you are on parchment. It's how old you feel in the humors.
jokes.setup: Burns arguing age doesn't matter
jokes.punchline: Using medieval terms 'parchment' and 'humors' to sound youthful
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns moment - using archaic language while claiming to be young at heart

jokes.text: Play along, chubsy. There's a pie in it for you.
jokes.setup: Burns asking Homer to vouch for his youthfulness
jokes.punchline: Bribing him with pie like a child
jokes.explanation: Burns treating Homer like a child while trying to appear youthful himself

jokes.text: He ran his own casino, stole the Loch Ness monster, got shot by a baby, and blotted out the sun!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make Burns sound exciting
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly absurd past Burns schemes
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating list of actual Burns schemes from show history, getting more ridiculous

jokes.text: These would stop Joan Collins herself!
jokes.setup: Homer putting on terrible underwear to avoid being attractive
jokes.punchline: Claiming they'd repel even the notoriously glamorous Joan Collins
jokes.explanation: Homer's confidence in his anti-sexy underwear using outdated sex symbol reference

jokes.text: Yes. That's totally voluntary.
jokes.setup: Gloria complimenting Burns' dancing
jokes.punchline: Burns clarifying his shaking isn't from old age
jokes.explanation: Burns' defensive clarification about his trembling reveals his insecurity about aging

jokes.text: You mean Young Man Burns.
jokes.setup: Homer calling him Old Man Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns' desperate correction to sound younger
jokes.explanation: Burns' vanity about age leads to sad attempt at rebranding himself

jokes.text: I'm going to make such love to you that you'll forget all about Rudolph Valentino.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to be romantic
jokes.punchline: Reference to silent film star from the 1920s
jokes.explanation: Burns' romantic talk references heartthrob from nearly a century ago

jokes.text: It was made from the pockets of the pocket fox, an animal that only existed for three weeks in the 16th century.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining his aphrodisiac
jokes.punchline: Elaborately fake origin story for extinct animal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd fake mythology for Burns' supposed love potion

jokes.text: Clean out your desk. You're gone.
jokes.setup: Lenny asking for raise during Burns' good mood
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately firing him instead
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - Burns' 'good mood' doesn't extend to employee relations

jokes.text: Did you hear that? That 'excellent' was excellent.
jokes.setup: Burns says his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Homer comments on the quality of Burns' catchphrase delivery
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about Burns' famous catchphrase. Self-referential but earned through character observation.

jokes.text: We have to... Expel some urine!
jokes.setup: Burns needing to speak privately with Smithers
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic announcement about using the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Burns' formal medical terminology for basic bodily function

jokes.text: My biological clock is ticking. I could be dead again soon.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining his rush to marry
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to having been dead before
jokes.explanation: Casual callback to Burns' deaths while parodying biological clock concept

jokes.text: I think you'll find that red ball more 'engaging.'
jokes.setup: Burns directing Gloria's attention to bowling ball
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'engaging' while proposing with ring inside ball
jokes.explanation: Solid pun setup for proposal scene, Burns' style of wordplay

jokes.text: Spectacular! Now we must celebrate. I'll get some Champagne-iola!
jokes.setup: Burns celebrating successful engagement
jokes.punchline: Made-up champagne brand with -ola suffix
jokes.explanation: Burns' tendency to add -ola to brand names, mild wordplay

jokes.text: I told the guard that I was going out for a pack of cigarettes. Then I totally stabbed him.
jokes.setup: Snake explaining his escape from prison
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of stabbing guard after mundane excuse
jokes.explanation: Snake's casual attitude toward violence fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Then I totally stabbed him.
jokes.setup: Snake explaining his prison escape method
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of stabbing a guard after a mundane setup
jokes.explanation: Snake's casual violence delivered matter-of-factly. The word 'totally' makes it more disturbing.

jokes.text: The cops were chasing us. I needed to lighten the load. And, um, protect you.
jokes.setup: Gloria asking why he pushed her from moving car
jokes.punchline: Snake's transparent attempt to make selfishness sound protective
jokes.explanation: Snake's obvious lie with awkward pause shows his manipulative nature

jokes.text: Let go of her, or I'll scream!
jokes.setup: Burns trying to be heroic
jokes.punchline: Threatening to scream instead of fight
jokes.explanation: Burns' physical frailty leads to feminine-coded threat instead of masculine action

jokes.text: Callooh! Callay! We're in luck! They had a magnum at the shoe counter.
jokes.setup: Burns returning with apparent weapon
jokes.punchline: Gets champagne bottle from bowling alley instead of gun
jokes.explanation: Burns' oblivious celebration while girlfriend is being kidnapped

jokes.text: Homer just comes along on our dates and carries us to the bedroom.
jokes.setup: Gloria explaining her relationship
jokes.punchline: Revealing Homer's awkward role in their romance
jokes.explanation: Disturbing image of Homer's involvement in Burns' romantic life

jokes.text: You're dating that old trilobite? Gross!
jokes.setup: Snake learning about Gloria's relationship
jokes.punchline: Calling Burns a prehistoric fossil
jokes.explanation: Creative insult comparing Burns to ancient marine creature

jokes.text: He was my sexually-virile best friend
jokes.setup: Burns describing Homer to news crew
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate description of Homer's sexual vitality
jokes.explanation: Burns' bizarre public characterization of Homer reveals his confused worldview

jokes.text: Well, according to our audience insta-poll, 46% say you're too old, and 37% say she's a skank.
jokes.setup: News anchor delivering poll results
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest public opinion presented as news
jokes.explanation: Satirizes modern media's crude instant polling and public judgment

jokes.text: I think his name was Gustafson.
jokes.setup: Homer asking who you have to kill for Snake's nice house
jokes.punchline: Snake casually providing actual murder victim's name
jokes.explanation: Snake's matter-of-fact response to rhetorical question about murder

jokes.text: You know what would be surprising? A foot massage.
jokes.setup: Gloria critiquing Snake's predictable violence
jokes.punchline: Suggesting foot massage as alternative to brass knuckles
jokes.explanation: Gloria's attempt to reform Snake through spa suggestions

jokes.text: Hey! Hey, I want that lawyer what wears the cowboy hat.
jokes.setup: Lou being arrested for helping police
jokes.punchline: Demands a specific lawyer known only by his hat
jokes.explanation: Specific detail about the cowboy hat lawyer makes this funnier than a generic demand for an attorney.

jokes.text: That's a sweet shot. He's tied to a chair.
jokes.setup: Eddie saying he can take out Snake
jokes.punchline: Lou pointing out the target is completely helpless
jokes.explanation: Lou's dry observation about how easy the shot would be

jokes.text: Well, we have an officer sneaking around the house, Kent, so unless they have a television in there or can hear my loud talking...
jokes.setup: Chief explaining tactical situation on TV
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's giving away police strategy on live television
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's dawning realization of his tactical blunder, excellent timing

jokes.text: I don't like bothering people at home.
jokes.setup: Snake explaining why he quit telemarketing
jokes.punchline: Violent criminal having moral qualms about phone calls
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - Snake has boundaries about phone etiquette but not violence

jokes.text: She's completely hairless, like those cats.
jokes.setup: Chief offering his sister as Burns replacement
jokes.punchline: Describing her like a Sphinx cat
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's inappropriate sales pitch comparing his sister to hairless cats

jokes.text: I don't care if she's Miss Hairless America! I'm spoken for.
jokes.setup: Burns rejecting Wiggum's sister
jokes.punchline: Creates absurd beauty pageant category from the hairless description
jokes.explanation: 'Miss Hairless America' is a perfect Burns response that builds on Wiggum's weird description.

jokes.text: You? No offense, but you're a decrepit monkey skeleton.
jokes.setup: Burns wanting to save Gloria himself
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's brutally honest physical assessment
jokes.explanation: Vivid and creative insult about Burns' frail appearance

jokes.text: Channel 6 is hot, hot, hot!
jokes.setup: Reporter asking traumatized woman to promote station
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate marketing request after rescue
jokes.explanation: Satirizes news media's shameless self-promotion even during tragedy

jokes.text: What about the wiener? A guy on TV lifted a can of paint with his.
jokes.setup: Burns saying heart is strongest muscle
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude counterargument from TV show
jokes.explanation: Homer ruining inspirational moment with inappropriate TV reference

jokes.text: You mean a Vandyke? No, a Vandyke has a moustache, doesn't it? I think it can. Are you talking about a soul patch? No! Wait. Maybe.
jokes.setup: Snake wanting to grow devil beard
jokes.punchline: Detailed discussion of facial hair terminology
jokes.explanation: Criminals getting sidetracked by grooming semantics, mild absurdity

jokes.text: You mean a Vandyke? No, a Vandyke has a moustache, doesn't it? I think it can. Are you talking about a soul patch?
jokes.setup: Discussion about devil beards and facial hair types
jokes.punchline: Increasingly technical discussion of beard terminology
jokes.explanation: Absurd ending where criminals debate facial hair terminology. Anticlimatic but character-appropriate confusion.
